article_title: Two Binges of Ethanol a Day Keep the Memory Away in Adolescent Rats: Key Role for GLUN2B Subunit

chunks_extracted.section: Bidirectional Synaptic Plasticity After Two Binges of Ethanol

aggregated_relations.subject: ethanol
evidence_sentences:
When recordings were performed 48h after EtOH, LTD was abolished at the end of the recording (EtOH48h: -3.6±3.2 %, n = 8, P=.002 compared with control) (Figure 2A
1-A2).

aggregated_relations.subject: ethanol
evidence_sentences:
LTD abolition was not obtained if the delay between treatment and recordings was increased to 8 days (control NaCl: -20.9±3.9% of baseline, n = 7, vs EtOH8d: -29.9±7.5, n = 6, P=.1) (Figure 2A
2-A4).

Effects of Ethanol on Basal Synaptic Transmission
Cellular Targets of 2 Binges of Ethanol During LTD Abolition

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: In contrast, ketamine abolished LTD and evoked similar effects as 2 binges of EtOH (Figure 7B1-B2; Ket 48 hours: -0.03±7.0, n = 6, P=.02 vs control group).
In contrast, ketamine abolished LTD and evoked similar effects as 2 binges of EtOH

aggregated_relations.subject: D-serine
evidence_sentences: Interestingly, D-serine administered after each binge of EtOH prevented LTD abolition 48 hours later (Figure 4C1-C2) (control NaCl: -18.0±4.3% of baseline, n=9 vs EtOH+D-serine 48 hours: -30.2±8.0, n = 5, P=.08).
Interestingly, D-serine administered after each binge of EtOH prevented LTD abolition 48 hours later

aggregated_relations.subject: 2 alcohol binges
evidence_sentences: Our major findings are: (1) cognitive deficits are induced 48 hours after only 2 alcohol binges; (2) ethanol-induced abolition of LTD in the hippocampus is observed at the same time when cognitive deficits occurred; (3) both cognitive and LTD deficits are prevented by D-serine and mimicked by ketamine; and (4) deficits seem to be linked to repetitive short-lasting NMDA receptor blockade only, inducing a change in subunit composition of the NMDA receptor.

chunks_extracted.section: Behavior Effects of Two Binges of Ethanol

aggregated_relations.subject: Two binges of ethanol
evidence_sentences: Two binges of ethanol induced cognitive deficits, since NOR was impaired.

aggregated_relations.subject: ethanol
evidence_sentences: More interestingly, animals were trained at a time when hippocampus LTD has been selectively suppressed by ethanol (see below).

aggregated_relations.subject: 2 i.p. doses of 2.5g/kg ethanol
evidence_sentences: In 7-day-old animals, 2 i.p. doses of 2.5g/kg ethanol given 2 hours apart impaired Y-maze performance (spontaneous alternation) without affecting water maze learning and retrieval 3 weeks later (Izumi et al., 2005a), although others found some disturbances (Wozniak et al., 2004).

aggregated_relations.subject: Four days of binge ethanol exposure
evidence_sentences: Four days of binge ethanol exposure in young adult rats impaired NOR 1 week after treatment (Cippitelli et al., 2010), suggesting that repetition of binges durably alter cognitive function.

aggregated_relations.subject: chronic methylphenidate treatment
evidence_sentences: Furthermore, 15 days after a chronic methylphenidate treatment but not the day after the treatment (Taukulis et al., 2014), object memory was impaired, suggesting that the effects of drugs of abuse develop during the abstinence period.

causal_tuples.evidence_sentence: Two binges of ethanol induced cognitive deficits, since NOR was impaired.

causal_tuples.subject: chronic methylphenidate treatment
causal_tuples.evidence_sentence: Furthermore, 15 days after a chronic methylphenidate treatment but not the day after the treatment (Taukulis et al., 2014), object memory was impaired, suggesting that the effects of drugs of abuse develop during the abstinence period.

evidence_sentences: D-serine is a pro-mnesiant drug and acute ethanol is known to block NMDA receptors (Möykkynen and Korpi, 2012).

chunks_extracted.section: Intermittence Reveals the Effects of Ethanol

aggregated_relations.subject: ethanol regimen
aggregated_relations.object: 500mg/dL BEC 1 hour after a second injection and/or a sustained 200-mg/dL level
evidence_sentences: In several studies, ethanol regimen induced 500mg/dL BEC 1 hour after a second injection and/or a sustained 200-mg/dL level for several hours (Collins et al., 1998; Crews et al., 2000; Izumi et al., 2005a).

aggregated_relations.subject: 2 independent intoxications
evidence_sentences: Therefore, our procedure can be interpreted as 2 independent intoxications inducing a repeated specific ethanol-related mechanism, leading to behavior and electrophysiological disturbances.
